preamble: The shift from [k] to [g] is a standard form of voicing lenition. It typically occurs in intervocalic positions where the voiceless stop gains voicing from the surrounding voiced vowels.

examples.note: Tamil intervocalic voicing: /k/ → [g] between vowels. E.g., Tamil 'makal' (daughter) realized as [magal]; Kannada 'beku' (need) → intervocalic [g] in casual speech.
Turkish intervocalic voicing: word-final /k/ → [g] when a vowel-initial suffix is attached. Same pattern: çocuk → çocuğu (child → its child).
Intervocalic voicing of the velar stop.

sources: Penny: A History of the Spanish Language
